message:
feat(lexical/cass-compat): zero-copy CassDocumentRef ingest path for large rebuilds

The existing `CassTantivyIndex::add_cass_documents` path takes a slice of
owned `CassDocument` values. That shape forces every caller on a hot
rebuild path to clone every per-conversation string field (agent,
workspace, source_path, title, source_id, origin_kind, origin_host) for
each message they index, even when those strings are already owned
upstream and never mutated between the borrow and the Tantivy submit.

Add a borrowed sibling so the rebuild pipeline can skip those clones:

- New `CassDocumentRef<'a>` struct mirrors `CassDocument` field-for-field
  but with `&'a str` / `Option<&'a str>` instead of `String` / `Option<String>`.
  It's `Copy` so rayon partitions split it cheaply.
- `CassTantivyIndex::add_cass_document_refs(&[CassDocumentRef<'_>])`
  reuses the same batch-planning logic (`cass_parallel_add_plan` with
  the existing writer-parallelism / batch-doc / min-docs / max-batches
  knobs) as `add_cass_documents`, but calls the new
  `build_cass_tantivy_document_ref` helper which constructs the
  `TantivyDocument` directly from `&str`/`&Option<&str>` references
  without producing any intermediate owned `String`.
- Preserves the existing `PARALLEL_PREP_THRESHOLD = 8` small-batch
  serialization behavior so sub-threshold batches don't pay the rayon
  setup cost.
- Re-export `CassDocumentRef` from `frankensearch-lexical::lib` and the
  workspace `crates/lib.rs` aggregator so downstream crates can name
  the borrowed type without reaching into `cass_compat` directly.

No behavior change for existing callers: `add_cass_documents` still
takes owned `CassDocument`s and goes through the same writer pipeline.
Callers that opt into the new entrypoint (e.g. CASS lexical rebuild)
avoid duplicating per-conversation metadata across every message they
index during a rebuild.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,12 +19,13 @@
 pub mod cass_compat;
 
 pub use cass_compat::{
-    CASS_SCHEMA_HASH, CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION, CassDocument, CassFields, CassMergeStatus,
-    CassQueryFilters, CassQueryToken, CassSourceFilter, CassTantivyIndex, CassWildcardPattern,
-    cass_build_preview, cass_build_schema, cass_build_tantivy_query, cass_ensure_tokenizer,
-    cass_fields_from_schema, cass_generate_edge_ngrams, cass_has_boolean_operators, cass_index_dir,
-    cass_open_search_reader, cass_parse_boolean_query, cass_regex_query_cached,
-    cass_regex_query_uncached, cass_sanitize_query, cass_schema_hash_matches,
+    CASS_SCHEMA_HASH, CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION, CassDocument, CassDocumentRef, CassFields,
+    CassMergeStatus, CassQueryFilters, CassQueryToken, CassSourceFilter, CassTantivyIndex,
+    CassWildcardPattern, cass_build_preview, cass_build_schema, cass_build_tantivy_query,
+    cass_ensure_tokenizer, cass_fields_from_schema, cass_generate_edge_ngrams,
+    cass_has_boolean_operators, cass_index_dir, cass_open_search_reader, cass_parse_boolean_query,
+    cass_regex_query_cached, cass_regex_query_uncached, cass_sanitize_query,
+    cass_schema_hash_matches,
 };
 
 // Re-export tantivy types that appear in frankensearch-lexical's public API.

@@ -19,7 +19,8 @@
 pub mod cass_compat;
 
 pub use cass_compat::{
-    CASS_SCHEMA_HASH, CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION, CassDocument, CassFields, CassMergeStatus,
+    CASS_SCHEMA_HASH, CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION, CassDocument, CassDocumentRef, CassFields,
+    CassMergeStatus,
     CassQueryFilters, CassQueryToken, CassSourceFilter, CassTantivyIndex, CassWildcardPattern,
     cass_build_preview, cass_build_schema, cass_build_tantivy_query, cass_ensure_tokenizer,
     cass_fields_from_schema, cass_generate_edge_ngrams, cass_has_boolean_operators, cass_index_dir,
